message:
Add comprehensive CI/CD pipeline to release workflow with automated GitHub releases

Transform dist.yml from a simple artifact builder into a complete CI/CD pipeline
that validates code quality, builds release artifacts, generates checksums, and
automatically publishes GitHub releases with all distribution files.

What changed:
- Added test job that runs before dist job to enforce quality gates
- Made dist job depend on test job completion (needs: test)
- Renamed artifact upload step for clarity (workflow vs release artifacts)
- Added automated GitHub Release creation with softprops/action-gh-release@v2
- Expanded test job to include all test suites (unit, E2E, installer tests)

New test job (comprehensive quality gates):
- Runs on: ubuntu-latest
- Checks out code with actions/checkout@v4
- Installs Rust nightly toolchain via dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
- Executes four validation steps:
  1. fmt: runs 'cargo fmt -- --check' to enforce code formatting
  2. clippy: runs 'cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings' to catch lints
  3. tests (unit + e2e): runs all test suites sequentially:
     - cargo test: unit tests, connector tests, storage tests, search tests
     - cargo test --test install_scripts -- --test-threads=1: installer E2E
     - cargo test --test e2e_index_tui -- --test-threads=1: index+TUI E2E
     - cargo test --test e2e_install_easy -- --test-threads=1: easy-mode installer E2E
- All E2E tests use --test-threads=1 for isolation (tempfile conflicts, lock files)
- Any failure in test job blocks dist job from running (fail-fast quality gate)

Dependency chain (needs: test):
- dist job now explicitly depends on test job completion via 'needs: test'
- If test job fails (fmt violations, clippy warnings, test failures), dist job
  never starts (prevents building/releasing broken code)
- Workflow execution order: test job → dist job → release creation
- This ensures only validated code is built and released

Artifact upload clarification:
- Renamed "Upload artifacts" to "Upload artifacts (workflow)" to distinguish
  workflow artifacts (internal CI storage) from release artifacts (public GitHub)
- Still uses actions/upload-artifact@v4 with same configuration
- Still uploads target/dist/**/* (all built artifacts and SHA256 files)
- Workflow artifacts remain accessible to subsequent workflow jobs and for
  debugging (45-day retention by default)

New GitHub Release creation:
- Uses softprops/action-gh-release@v2 for automated release publishing
- Configuration:
  * name: ${{ github.ref_name }} (uses tag name as release title, e.g., "v0.1.0")
  * draft: false (publishes release immediately, not as draft)
  * prerelease: false (marks as stable release, not pre-release)
  * files: target/dist/* (uploads all distribution artifacts to release)
  * env: GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} (uses GitHub-provided token)
- Triggered by tag push (on: push: tags: ['v*']) as defined in workflow triggers
- Creates release for the tag that triggered the workflow
- Uploads all files from target/dist/ including:
  * Platform tarballs: coding-agent-search-vX.Y.Z-{linux,darwin,windows}-{x86_64,arm64}.{tar.gz,zip}
  * SHA256 checksums: *.tar.gz.sha256, *.zip.sha256
  * Any additional cargo-dist artifacts (installers, Homebrew formula, etc.)

Release automation benefits:
- Eliminates manual release creation via GitHub web UI
- Ensures all built artifacts are attached to release automatically
- SHA256 checksums are included in release for installer verification
- Consistent release naming (always matches tag name)
- Release notes can be added manually after automated creation or via future
  changelog automation

Token permissions:
- Uses ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} which is automatically provided by GitHub Actions
- Token has permissions to create releases and upload assets to the repository
- No additional secrets or PAT tokens required
- Token is scoped to the workflow run (expires after workflow completes)

Workflow trigger (unchanged):
- Still triggers on push of tags matching 'v*' pattern (e.g., v0.1.0, v1.2.3)
- Tag push initiates: test job → dist job → release creation sequence
- Developer workflow: git tag v0.1.0 && git push origin v0.1.0

Quality assurance flow:
1. Developer pushes tag: git push origin v0.1.0
2. Workflow triggers and test job starts
3. Test job runs fmt, clippy, all unit tests, all E2E tests
4. If any test fails: workflow stops, no artifacts built, no release created
5. If all tests pass: dist job starts
6. Dist job builds release artifacts via cargo-dist
7. Dist job generates SHA256 checksums for all artifacts
8. Dist job uploads artifacts as workflow artifacts (for debugging)
9. GitHub Release is created with tag name
10. All artifacts from target/dist/ are uploaded to the release
11. Installer scripts can now download artifacts from GitHub release

Test coverage in CI:
- Code formatting (cargo fmt) ensures consistent style
- Linting (cargo clippy -D warnings) catches potential bugs and anti-patterns
- Unit tests validate core functionality (connectors, storage, indexer, search)
- Connector tests verify all six agent log parsers with fixtures
- Storage tests validate SQLite schema, migrations, FTS mirroring
- Search tests verify Tantivy indexing and query correctness
- Indexer tests validate incremental ingestion and watch mode
- TUI tests validate headless mode with buffer snapshots
- Installer E2E tests validate both bash and PowerShell installers
- Index+TUI E2E test validates full-stack integration
- Easy-mode installer E2E test validates rustup bootstrap simulation

Artifact availability:
- Release artifacts are publicly accessible via GitHub Releases UI
- Checksums are published alongside artifacts for installer verification
- Install scripts auto-fetch checksums from release assets
- No manual checksum copy-paste required (automated in workflow)

Error handling:
- If test job fails: dist job skipped, no release created, workflow marked failed
- If dist job fails: release not created, workflow marked failed
- If release creation fails: workflow marked failed, artifacts still in workflow
  storage for manual download/debugging
- softprops/action-gh-release@v2 is idempotent: re-running workflow after fixing
  issues will update existing release or create if missing

Future enhancements enabled:
- Can add changelog generation before release creation
- Can add Homebrew formula update automation
- Can add Docker image publishing as additional dist job step
- Can add release notes generation from commit messages or PR titles
- Can add artifact signing with GPG or minisign

This enhancement automates the complete release pipeline from code validation
through artifact building to public release publishing, ensuring high quality
releases and reducing manual release management overhead.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -7,7 +7,24 @@ on:
       - 'v*'
 
 jobs:
+  test:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
+      - name: fmt
+        run: cargo fmt -- --check
+      - name: clippy
+        run: cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
+      - name: tests (unit + e2e)
+        run: |
+          cargo test
+          cargo test --test install_scripts -- --test-threads=1
+          cargo test --test e2e_index_tui -- --test-threads=1
+          cargo test --test e2e_install_easy -- --test-threads=1
+
   dist:
+    needs: test
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     steps:
       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
@@ -25,8 +42,18 @@ jobs:
           find target/dist -type f ! -name "*.sha256" -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
             sha256sum "$f" > "$f.sha256";
           done
-      - name: Upload artifacts
+      - name: Upload artifacts (workflow)
         uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
         with:
           name: dist-artifacts
           path: target/dist/**/*
+      - name: Create GitHub Release
+        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
+        with:
+          name: ${{ github.ref_name }}
+          draft: false
+          prerelease: false
+          files: |
+            target/dist/*
+        env:
+          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
